aquacroptools 0.2.0

    • Regional Crop Simulations Using aquacroptools

Parse an AquaCrop Calendar (.CAL) File

Source: R/read_cal.R, R/readers.R
read_cal.Rd

Reads a .CAL file and returns a structured list of onset parameters. Supports fixed onset, rainfall criteria 1-6, and thermal criteria 1-4.

Reads a .CAL file and returns a structured list of onset parameters.

read_cal(file)

read_cal(file)

Arguments

file

Character. Full path to the .CAL file.

Value

A named list with elements:

onset

Character. "fixed", "rainfall", or "thermal".

fixed_day

Integer or NA.

window_start

Integer or NA.

window_length

Integer or NA.

criterion_internal

Integer or NA.

criterion

Integer or NA. User-facing number (1-6 rainfall, 1-4 thermal).

preset_value

Numeric or NA.

successive_days

Integer or NA.

occurrences

Integer or NA.

rday

Integer or NA. Criterion 5 only.

dspell

Integer or NA. Criterion 5 only.

min_weekly_rain

Numeric or NA. Criterion 6 only.

spell_days

Integer or NA. Criterion 6 only.

lookahead_days

Integer or NA. Criteria 5 and 6.

A named list with elements:

onset

Character. "fixed", "rainfall", or "thermal".

fixed_day

Integer or NA. Fixed calendar day (onset "fixed" only).

window_start

Integer or NA.

window_length

Integer or NA.

criterion_internal

Integer or NA. AquaCrop internal criterion number.

criterion

Integer or NA. User-facing criterion number (1-4).

preset_value

Numeric or NA.

successive_days

Integer or NA. NA when not applicable.

occurrences

Integer or NA.

Details

Criterion 5 (generalised Sivakumar) carries three extra fields: rday, dspell, and lookahead_days.

Criterion 6 (Marteau 2009) carries three extra fields: min_weekly_rain, spell_days, and lookahead_days.

All other criteria return NA for these fields.

See also

Other AquaCrop readers: read_cli(), read_eto(), read_plu(), read_tnx()

Other AquaCrop readers: read_cli(), read_eto(), read_plu(), read_tnx()

Examples

if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
# Standard rainfall criterion
cal <- read_cal("CAL/station_01.CAL")
cal$onset        # "rainfall"
cal$criterion    # 2
cal$preset_value # 30

# Sivakumar criterion
cal5 <- read_cal("CAL/station_siv.CAL")
cal5$criterion      # 5
cal5$rday           # 1
cal5$dspell         # 7
cal5$lookahead_days # 30

# Marteau criterion
cal6 <- read_cal("CAL/station_mrt.CAL")
cal6$criterion       # 6
cal6$min_weekly_rain # 5
cal6$spell_days      # 7
cal6$lookahead_days  # 20
} # }
if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
cal <- read_cal("CAL/station_01.CAL")
cal$onset        # "rainfall"
cal$criterion    # 2
cal$preset_value # 30
} # }
